perturbation.original_text: The Initial Term shall automatically renew for one-month periods thereafter unless either party provides 30- days advance notice of termination, unless earlier terminated pursuant to Section 2.2 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Initial Term shall automatically renew for one-month periods thereafter only if both parties provide a written confirmation for renewal. Otherwise, the agreement will terminate automatically after the Initial Term.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement automatically renews unless either party provides a 30-day notice of termination. The changed text requires *both* parties to provide written confirmation for renewal; otherwise, the agreement terminates. This creates a contradiction because automatic renewal is no longer the default, requiring affirmative action from both parties instead of just the absence of a termination notice. This introduces uncertainty in enforcement, as it's unclear whether the contract auto-renews or needs an explicit agreement, which is contradicted in Section 2.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date, and shall continue thereafter for a period of twelve (12) months (the "Initial Term"). The Initial Term shall automatically renew for one-month periods thereafter only if both parties provide a written confirmation for renewal. Otherwise, the agreement will terminate automatically after the Initial Term. For purposes hereof, the Initial Term, together with any extension or renewal terms, shall hereinafter be collectively referred to as the "Term".
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding the renewal of the agreement. The original text allowed for automatic renewal unless either party provided notice of termination. The changed text requires *both* parties to provide written confirmation for renewal. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, reinforces this new requirement. The change necessitates affirmative action (written confirmation by both parties) for renewal, conflicting with the original automatic renewal process, therefore creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to Developer's expressly granted rights under this Agreement, Developer acknowledges and agrees that DSS shall own all right, title, and interest in and to the Technology, the Improvements, its Intellectual Property, and all future derivative works derived therefrom or developed hereunder. Developer agrees that it will not at any time (i) do or cause to be done any act or thing contesting or in any way impairing any part of such right, title and interest or (ii) represent, expressly or by implication that it has any right, title or interest in or to any of the foregoing other than as expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to Developer's expressly granted rights under this Agreement, Developer acknowledges and agrees that DSS shall own all right, title, and interest in and to the Technology, its Intellectual Property, and all future derivative works derived therefrom or developed hereunder. Developer retains ownership of the Improvements developed hereunder and can commercialize them independently. Developer agrees that it will not at any time (i) do or cause to be done any act or thing contesting or in any way impairing any part of such right, title and interest or (ii) represent, expressly or by implication that it has any right, title or interest in or to any of the foregoing other than as expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 3.1, the original text explicitly states that DSS owns all rights to the Technology, Improvements, Intellectual Property, and derivative works. The changed text now carves out an exception, stating that Developer retains ownership of the 'Improvements' developed. This contradicts the original statement that DSS owns all 'Improvements,' creating uncertainty about who owns the 'Improvements' and who has the right to commercialize them. This is further amplified by the following sentence that refers to the previous as well, thus adding in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Developer hereby acknowledges DSS's claim of sole ownership of the Technology, the Improvements, and all associated goodwill. Nothing in this Agreement or in the performance thereof, or that might otherwise be implied by law, shall operate to grant Developer any right, title, or interest in or to the Technology or the Improvements. Developer hereby assigns and shall assign in the future to DSS all rights it may acquire by operation of law or otherwise in the Technology or Improvements, along with the goodwill associated therewith. DSS shall have the sole right to, and in its sole discretion may, commence, prosecute or defend, and control any legal action concerning the Technology and Improvements. Developer may not contest the validity of, by act or omission jeopardize, or take any action inconsistent with, DSS's ownership rights or goodwill in the Technology or Improvements, including any attempted registration of the Technology or Improvements in Hong Kong or in any other legal jurisdiction, or any attempts to license the same to any unauthorized third Person.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that DSS owns all rights to the Technology, Improvements, Intellectual Property, and derivative works. The changed text contradicts this by stating that the Developer retains ownership of the 'Improvements' developed. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which explicitly states, 'Developer hereby acknowledges DSS's claim of sole ownership of the Technology, the Improvements...Nothing in this Agreement...shall operate to grant Developer any right, title, or interest in or to the...Improvements.' This creates an in-text contradiction regarding ownership of the Improvements.

perturbation.original_text: During the Term of this Agreement and thereafter for a period of five (5) years, the parties shall be bound by all of the protective terms and conditions of the NDA.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term of this Agreement and thereafter for a period of ten (10) years, the parties shall only be bound by sections 1-5 of the protective terms and conditions of the NDA. The other sections may be reviewed and renegotiated after the termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the NDA's protective terms and conditions remain in effect for five years after the agreement's term. The revised text extends this period to ten years but states that only sections 1-5 of NDA apply. The addition of only sections 1-5 applying creates a contradiction because the original sentence states 'all' the conditions. Also the statement that says: The other sections may be reviewed and renegotiated after the termination. Indicates that some NDA terms may not be binding which introduces uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that they have entered into that certain Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement dated as of January 18, 2018 (the "NDA"), a copy of which is attached hereto as Exhibit A. The terms of the NDA shall be deemed to be incorporated by reference into this Agreement, mutatis mutandis. During the Term of this Agreement and thereafter for a period of ten (10) years, the parties shall only be bound by sections 1-5 of the protective terms and conditions of the NDA. The other sections may be reviewed and renegotiated after the termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'all' of the protective terms and conditions of the NDA would remain in effect for five years. The changed text extends the period to ten years, but limits the application to only sections 1-5 of the NDA's protective terms. This creates a contradiction because the original NDA was referencing 'all' terms, while the modified text limits this to sections 1-5. The statement 'The other sections may be reviewed and renegotiated after the termination' introduces further uncertainty and contradicts the initial incorporation by reference. This uncertainty and limitation creates a direct conflict and contradiction to the incorporated NDA.
